_readme: Published worked-example anchors for the nodal engine (CASE 8).
Owner authorized arming from web-sourced verification on 2026-07-19
(previously owner-provided book pages only). Each fixture records its
source, verification level and tolerance rationale. verification:
'book-text' = numbers read from a full-text copy of the book itself;
validate against the book, not from the book text directly.

fixtures.source: Guo, Lyons & Ghalambor, Petroleum Production Engineering (Elsevier 2007), Example 4.5 (AverageTZ.xls, Table 4.4)
fixtures.notes: Book zbar 0.9027 (Brill-Beggs class) vs our Papay route 0.909; the 0.5% gate absorbs the z difference.

fixtures.source: Guo, Lyons & Ghalambor, Petroleum Production Engineering (Elsevier 2007), Example 4.6 (Cullender-Smith.xls, Table 4.5)
Brill & Mukherjee, Multiphase Flow in Wells (SPE Monograph 17, 1999), Example 2.2 (classic Cullender-Smith 10,000 ft data set)
fixtures.notes: High-Ppr column: the book's Standing-Katz z (~0.79 at wellhead) vs our Papay route (~0.77) accounts for ~1.5%; gated at 2% with the z-route caveat. Book Itf 189.41, ours 193.7.

fixtures.source: Norhazilah Abdul Rahman, UTP thesis (utpedia 10676), manual Cullender-Smith calculation following Economides, Petroleum Production Systems
fixtures.notes: Thesis-quality source with OCR noise; every step arithmetically cross-checked during research. Simpson pwf printed as 2175 + 6*83.09/(0.169+4*0.172+0.175).

fixtures.source: Lyons & Plisga (eds.), Standard Handbook of Petroleum & Natural Gas Engineering 2nd ed (2005), Example 6.2.5 pp. 6-85/6-86 (original Beggs & Brill, no Payne)
fixtures.notes: Gates the pre-Payne holdup chain and the friction-ratio quartic against the book's printed intermediates (book rounds each step). The book's fn=0.0271 and its Ek are internally inconsistent (its own smooth-pipe formula gives 0.0162; Ek uses p in psia), so the full-gradient chain is deliberately NOT gated.

fixtures.source: rNodal (f0nzie), interp.fanbr(drhov=10, GLR=3200) = 0.009646559, degree-4 log-log fit of the Brown TALM Fig. 2.41 digitization
fixtures.notes: Cross-implementation check, not a book gate: we interpolate the same digitized points piecewise log-log linearly; rNodal fits a degree-4 polynomial. ~1% divergence between the two treatments is expected.

fixtures.source: Takacs, Gas Lift Manual (PennWell 2005), Example 2.24 (via PressureDrop.jl / sksite05 regression tests)
fixtures.notes: NOT ARMED: the example sits at X1 = 7.12e-5 where the book's chart read (HL/psi = 0.44) diverges from both standard published fit families (Economides/Trina and Guo log-polynomial both give ~0.28). Our engine matches the industry-standard fits (0.117 psi/ft vs the book's 0.160). Arms only if the owner re-anchors the low-X1 chart region against the original Hagedorn & Brown figure.

fixtures.source: SPE Petroleum Engineering Handbook Vol. IV (PetroWiki 'Flow through chokes'), worked example: 400 STB/D, GLR 800 scf/STB, 12/64 choke
SPE Petroleum Engineering Handbook Vol. IV, same example via the Ros coefficients
fixtures.notes: PEH Table 1.9 prints A1 = 4.26e-3 (implies C = 17.45); we carry the canonical C = 17.40 (Guo Table 5.3, PIPESIM), giving 1367 vs the printed 1371 - 0.28%.

fixtures.source: Guo, Lyons & Ghalambor (2007) Example 5.1: 0.6 SG gas, 1-in orifice choke, 800 -> 200 psia, 75 F, k 1.3, CD 0.62
fixtures.notes: Book rounds intermediates; our exact evaluation gives 12,753 (0.08%). Downstream temperature 5 F reproduces the book's icing warning.

fixtures.source: Guo, Lyons & Ghalambor (2007) Example 5.2: 0.65 SG gas, 1.5-in nozzle choke, 100 -> 80 psia, 70 F, k 1.25, CD 1.2
Guo, Lyons & Ghalambor (2007) Example 5.3 (GasUpChokePressure.xls, Table 5.1): 5,000 Mscf/d against 300 psia through 32/64, CD 0.99
Guo, Lyons & Ghalambor (2007) Table 5.4: Sachdeva (SPE 15657) subcritical two-phase example, 24/64 choke, 80 -> 50 psia, CD 0.75, printed chain to 300.56 bbl/d liquid
fixtures.notes: NOT ARMED: the Sachdeva G2 mass-flux equation as reproduced in secondary sources does not close the book's own printed chain (checked by hand: factor ~3.2 in the bracket); the module ships critical-flow screening only until SPE 15657 is transcribed from the primary text. All printed Table 5.4 numbers are preserved in the research notes for that pass.
NA3 target: gasLiftSweep optimum matches the printed optimum injection rate.
